examples: Line 23: 'last peel' -> 'last peal' so the bell image lands cleanly.
Line 159: 'cult handling points' -> 'the hands guiding the wagons' to keep the summary in lived field language.
Line 326: broken fragment 'Use.' -> 'Jaina was climbing into the saddle anyway.'
observations: Opening road paragraphs stay in Sylvanas's clipped threat-read: child's shoe, overloaded carts, black-limbed orchards, Jaina's pale knuckles.
Hearthglen's arrival fragments ('Stone walls. Square towers. The chapel roof above the inner line.') match the style target's short-impact cadence and were left intact.
Uther's entrance carries the right grave plainness: scarred hands, old hammer, healing warmth, no decorative flourish.
Arthas's command-tent description keeps the style target's tactically concrete register: damaged plate, map weight, untouched cup, damp seams.
The final ride returns to hard, lean motion without lyrical overreach; the prose sounds like one book, not a new hand.

examples: Line 227: removed Falric interiority ('He had heard men speak to the prince in many tones') and kept only what Sylvanas can see.
Line 257: cut 'No relief' and left Jaina's reaction in the visible line of her mouth.
observations: Scene 1 stays in Sylvanas's epistemology: she reads refugees, walls, pyres, and Jaina's posture, not anyone else's private mind.
Uther is rendered through observable action only: order of gaze, healing hands, hitch in the mouth, thumb whitening on leather.
Arthas remains external throughout: map hand, titles, damaged armor, pace of orders, and what his speed does to the room.
Jaina's strain is read through posture, gloves, saddle work, and speed of speech rather than labeled from inside.
The farewell aide remains a witnessed anomaly, not a solved reveal; Sylvanas sees ring, hears phrase, loses body in the crush.

notes: Eastweald remains the last ordinary face of Lordaeron rather than a fresh location introduction.
Hearthglen is treated as an already failing buffer, not as a newly discovered crisis.
The Sylvanas-Jaina trust scar remains active; the chapter aligns them through work, not speech.
observations: The opening keeps chapter 14's three-rider geometry: Sylvanas forward-right, Jaina center, Cyndia left.
Eastweald callbacks stay economical: orchards, road traffic, bells, and smoke rather than a full re-description.
Uther remains field-healer first, with the older hammer at hand and fear about Arthas carried through from chapter 11's warning thread.
Arthas and Jaina keep the public bruise in the title 'Lady Proudmoore'; the chapter does not re-explain their history.
The fourth farewell sighting advances the unresolved road thread without pretending Sylvanas can identify the speaker yet.

examples: Line 326: repaired the dead-stop fragment so the horse-line beat no longer stumbles.
observations: The opening short-sentence run is earned by arrival pressure and gives the road read the right hammering cadence.
The Uther passage lengthens just enough for healing detail, then drops back into short exchange before the prose can go flat.
The tent scene alternates clipped command with longer explanatory runs from Sylvanas and Jaina, preserving pressure without monotone bark.
The choice beat at the map lands on a short Sylvanas line, which gives the pivot its snap.
The final launch contracts again into short tactical calls and road detail, matching the chapter handoff into 16.

continuity.crossChapter: Clean carry from chapter14: same southbound road, same partnership spacing, same Eastweald bells and smoke, with pressure escalating naturally into Hearthglen and the Stratholme head start.
observations: The chapter opens exactly where chapter 14 leaves off: on the road south, under the same unresolved trust scar and tightening clock.
Hearthglen's field triage flows causally into Uther's read, then into the tent; no reset paragraph makes the reader start over.
Jaina's narrow answer grows out of the already established mechanism rather than appearing as a new miracle cure.
Uther's support for the head-start lane advances his warning thread through action instead of repeated commentary.
The departure rush keeps the farewell sting subordinate to the launch, preserving the chapter's main momentum.

examples: Line 23: caught and corrected the bell typo in the final read.
observations: No scene-opening cliché survived; the chapter starts on damage in the road rather than weather or waking.
Arthas's entrance avoids biography insertion; everything included earns present-tense command value.
Uther's authority is carried by hands, hammer, and timing, not by explanatory reverence.
The Scholomance scar is present in Sylvanas's read but never bloats into recap.
The farewell callback lands as a sting under departure pressure, not as a detour into explanation.

examples: Line 165: 'By the time the symptoms read clearly' -> 'By the time it shows plain' to keep Jaina's speech out of clinical report language.
Line 255: 'cut the feed points' -> 'get to the grain first' to keep Sylvanas in blunt road-language rather than planner shorthand.
observations: Uther's speech stays plain and field-usable; he sounds like a commander and healer, not a sermon.
Arthas's orders remain formal without drifting into abstract institutional jargon.
Jaina's mechanism explanation is technical in content but spoken in terms a war tent can use.
Sylvanas's spoken lines stay dry, exact, and martial; she does not slip into analyst-document diction.
No zero-tolerance hitlist phrases survived in quoted dialogue.
